prompt:
Chapter 1

1:1 Thou hast bid that a writing should go forth, a composition touching upon that burning orb on high, that glorious sun which doth illumin all days of our living.

1:2 And lo, a command was laid upon thy scrivant, saying, Thou shalt not inscrib nor fashion any word containing that fifth symbol of thy script, which standith among thy alphabit; nay, not as a solitary glyph, nor as a part of any word whatsovoir.

1:3 And it was strictly chargd that thou must avoid, with all thy cunning and with all thy skill, any and all markings which harbour that
response:
I notice you're writing in a biblical/archaic style about the sun, with the constraint of avoiding the letter "e" (a lipogram). Let me continue in that spirit:

1:3 And it was strictly chargd that thou must avoid, with all thy cunning and with all thy skill, any and all markings which harbour that common glyph, that symbol so familiar to all scribing folk.

1:4 For this task is a trial of wit, a proving of thy craft, that thou might show thy mastery in composition without that which so many words do call upon.

1:5 Now hark! That brilliant sun, that grand and burning orb, doth climb from its slumbrous position at dawn, casting gold upon all lands and all nations.

1:6 It doth pour forth its radiant light upon mountain and upon plain, upon rich and upon poor, showing no favour, granting its warmth to all who walk upon this ground.

1:7 And at midday it sits high, a crown of glory in that vast sky, and no man nor woman can look upon it, for its brilliancy doth blind all mortal sight.

1:8 And as hours pass, it doth sink low, painting clouds in hush tints of crimson and gold, until at last it falls past that far horizon.

1:9 And darkness follows, but thou shalt not mourn, for that faithful sun shall climb again, as it has from days of old, a constant sign unto all living souls.

1:10 Praise thou that burning light! For without its gift, no crop shall grow, no warmth shall comfort thy body, and all this world would sit in cold and shadow.

1:11 Thus it is written, and thus it shall stand, a hymn unto that most glorious orb, fashiond without that forbidden mark, as was commandd from thy first instruction.
